text: And when the men of Israel were gone away, he stood and smote the Philistines till his hand was weary, and grew stiff with the sword: and the Lord wrought a great victory that day: and the people that were fled away, returned to take spoils of them that were slain.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after him was Semma the son of Age of Arari. And the Philistines were gathered together in a troop: for there was a field full of lentils. And when the people were fled from the face of the Philistines,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He stood in the midst of the field, and defended it, and defeated the Philistines: and the Lord gave a great victory.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moreover also before this the three who were princes among the thirty, went down and came to David in the harvest time into the cave of Odollam: and the camp of the Philistines was in the valley of the giants.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And David was then in a hold: and there was a garrison of the Philistines then in Bethlehem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And David longed, and said: O that some man would get me a drink of the water out of the cistern, that is in Bethlehem, by the gate.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the three valiant men broke through the camp of the Philistines, and drew water out of the cistern of Bethlehem, that was by the gate, and brought it to David: but he would not drink, but offered it to the Lord,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Saying: The Lord be merciful to me, that I may not do this: shall I drink the blood of these men that went, and the peril of their lives? therefore he would not drink. These things did these three mighty men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Abisai also the brother of Joab, the son of Sarvia, was chief among three: and he lifted up his spear against three hundred whom he slew, and he was renowned among the three,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the noblest of three, and was their chief, but to the three first he attained not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Banaias the son of Joiada a most valiant man, of great deeds, of Cabseel: he slew the two lions of Moab, and he went down, and slew a lion in the midst of a pit, in the time of snow.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He also slew an Egyptian, a man worthy to be a sight, having a spear in his hand: but he went down to him with a rod, and forced the spear out of the hand of the Egyptian, and slew him with his own spear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These things did Banaias the son of Joiada.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he was renowned among the three valiant men, who were the most honourable among the thirty: but he attained not to the first three: and David made him of his privy council.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Asael the brother of Joab was one of the thirty, Elehanan the son of Dodo of Bethlehem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Semma of Harodi, Elica of Harodi,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Heles of Phalti, Hira the son of Acces of Thecua,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Abiezer of Anathoth, Mobonnai of Husati,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Selmon the Ahohite, Maharai the Netophathite,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Heled the son of Baana, also a Netophathite, Ithai the son of Ribai of Gabaath of the children of Benjamin,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Banaia the Pharathonite, Heddai of the torrent Gaas,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Abialbon the Arbathite, Azmaveth of Beromi,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Eliaba of Salaboni. The sons of Jassen, Jonathan,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Semma of Orori, Aliam the son of Sarar the Arorite,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Eliphelet the son of Aasbai the son of Machati, Eliam the son of Achitophel the Gelonite,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hesrai of Carmel, Pharai of Arbi,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Igaal the son of Nathan of Soba, Bonni of Gadi,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Selec of Ammoni, Naharai the Berothite, armourbearer of Joab the son of Sarvia,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Ira the Jethrite, Gareb also a Jethrite;

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Urias the Hethite, thirty and seven in all.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the anger of the Lord was again kindled against Israel, and stirred up David among them, saying: Go, number Israel and Juda.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the king said to Joab the general of his army: Go through all the tribes of Israel from Dan to Bersabee, and number ye the people that I may know the number of them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Joab said to the king: The Lord thy God increase thy people, and make them as many more as they are now, and again multiply them a hundredfold in the sight of my lord the king: but what meaneth my lord the king by this kind of thing?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the king’s words prevailed over the words of Joab, and of the captains of the army: and Joab, and the captains of the soldiers went out from the presence of the king, to number the people of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they had passed the Jordan, they came to Aroer to the right side of the city, which is in the vale of Gad.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And by Jazer they passed into Galaad, and to the lower land of Hodsi, and they came into the woodlands of Dan. And going about by Sidon,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They passed near the walls of Tyre, and all the land of the Hevite, and the Chanaanite, and they came to the south of Juda into Bersabee:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And having gone through the whole land, after nine months and twenty days, they came to Jerusalem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Joab gave up the sum of the number of the people to the king, and there were found of Israel eight hundred thousand valiant men that drew the sword: and of Juda five hundred thousand fighting men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But David’s heart struck him, after the people were numbered: and David said to the Lord: I have sinned very much in what I have done: but I pray thee, O Lord, to take away the iniquity of thy servant, because I have done exceeding foolishly.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And David arose in the morning, and the word of the Lord came to Gad the prophet and the seer of David, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Go, and say to David: Thus saith the Lord: I give thee thy choice of three things, choose one of them which thou wilt, that I may do it to thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Gad was come to David, he told him, saying: Either seven years of famine shall come to thee in thy land: or thou shalt flee three months before thy adversaries, and they shall pursue thee: or for three days there shall be a pestilence in thy land. Now therefore deliberate, and see what answer I shall return to him that sent me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And David said to Gad: I am in a great strait: but it is better that I should fall into the hands of the Lord (for his mercies are many) than into the hands of men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord sent a pestilence upon Israel, from the morning unto the time appointed, and there died of the people from Dan to Bersabee seventy thousand men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the angel of the Lord had stretched out his hand over Jerusalem to destroy it, the Lord had pity on the affliction, and said to the angel that slew the people: It is enough: now hold thy hand. And the angel of the Lord was by the thrashingfloor of Areuna the Jebusite.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And David said to the Lord, when he saw the angel striking the people: It is I; I am he that have sinned, I have done wickedly: these that are the sheep, what have they done? let thy hand, I beseech thee, be turned against me, and against my father’s house.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Gad came to David that day, and said: Go up, and build an altar to the Lord in the thrashingfloor of Areuna the Jebusite.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And David went up according to the word of Gad which the Lord had commanded him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Areuna looked, and saw the king and his servants coming towards him:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And going out he worshipped the king, bowing with his face to the earth, and said: Wherefore is my lord the king come to his servant? And David said to him: To buy the thrashingfloor of thee, and build an altar to the Lord, that the plague, which rageth among the people, may cease.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Areuna said to David: Let my lord the king take, and offer, as it seemeth good to him: thou hast here oxen for a holocaust, and the wain, and the yokes of the oxen for wood.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All these things Areuna as a king gave to the king: and Areuna said to the king: The Lord thy God receive thy vow.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the king answered him, and said: Nay, but I will buy it of thee, at a price, and I will not offer to the Lord my God holocausts free cost. So David bought the floor, and the oxen, for fifty sicles of silver:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And David built there an altar to the Lord, and offered holocausts and peace offerings: and the Lord became merciful to the land, and the plague was stayed from Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
